title: Consequences

quoteFirst: “Admiral made telegraph signal: ‘Send two boats to the _Acasta_ to assist in bringing off prisoners.’ At
quoteLast: , observed the wrecks to be on fire.”
title: Jane Austen's sailor brothers
author: Hubback, J. H. (John Henry), 1844-; Hubback, Edith C. (Edith Charlotte), 1876-1945

quoteFirst: of 80° F. The petiole fell from 8 A.M. till 10.15 A.M., it then rose a little in a somewhat zigzag line, often remaining stationary, till 5 P.M., when the great evening fall commenced, which was continued till at least 10 P.M. By 7 A.M. on the following morning it had risen to the same level as on the previous morning, and then descended in a zigzag line. But from 10.30 A.M. till
quoteLast: it remained almost motionless, all power of movement being now lost. The petiole, therefore, of this very old leaf, which must have long ceased growing, moved periodically; but instead of circumnutating several times during the day, it moved only twice down and twice up in the course of 24 h.
title: The Power of Movement in Plants
author: Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882; Darwin, Francis, Sir, 1848-1925

quoteFirst: His working hours were from seven till four o’clock, but, since the measure he gave was always good, he seldom left the apartments till nearer five. To-day, however, there had come no visitors to interrupt his labours, and by a
quoteLast: there was no more to be conveniently done.
title: And Five Were Foolish
author: Yates, Dornford, 1885-1960

quoteFirst: The guide seemed quite delighted to see me so much pleased, and took care, in descending, to lead me to the edge of every precipice, which he had not done in going up. I, however, presently recollected, that I was in a great hurry to get back, and set off along the brink of the cavity for the highest peak, where I arrived at
quoteLast: , and saw a view, of which it is impossible to form any idea from description. For many miles around, it was composed of tops of high mountains, of all the various forms that can be imagined: some appeared swimming in an ocean of vapour; on others, the clouds lay like a cap of snow, appearing as soft as down.
title: The Book of Curiosities
author: Platts, John, 1775-1837

quoteLast: to the minute, there came a most genteel little tap-tappity. "Is Mrs. Ribston at home?" inquired Duchess in the porch.
title: The Tale of the Pie and the Patty Pan

title: The Battle of the Falkland Islands, Before and After
author: Spencer-Cooper, Henry Edmund Harvey, 1884-1968
